finding: Fresh, uniquely selected JWKS material is insufficient unless the verifier also applies an application-owned JOSE header policy that binds the token algorithm to the selected key and rejects remote key indirection, unsupported critical headers, algorithm confusion, and wrong token types before signature verification.

primary_sources.relevance: Requires caller-controlled algorithm allowlists, exact key-to-algorithm binding, validation of cryptographic inputs, issuer/audience validation, and defenses against remote key lookup and cross-JWT confusion.
Defines JOSE algorithms and key-type semantics used by the policy evaluator.
Documents GitHub Actions OIDC tokens as JWTs and shows typ JWT, alg RS256, kid, issuer, audience, and run-bound claims.

verification.route: node --test github-oidc-jose-header-policy.test.mjs in an isolated Node runtime using byte-identical files
covered: exact RS256 header/key binding accepted without claiming signature verification
none, HMAC confusion, and non-allowlisted algorithms rejected
jku, x5u, and unsupported crit handling rejected fail-closed
kid, alg, kty, use, and key_ops mismatches rejected

uncertainty: The evaluator validates policy compatibility only; it does not parse JWT bytes, verify a cryptographic signature, validate claims, prove token origin, or authorize a deployment.
falsification_route: Present a GitHub-issued token or supported key rotation case that cannot pass while satisfying the documented RS256/JWT contract, or show that an accepted header/key pair enables algorithm substitution, remote key injection, unsupported critical semantics, or incompatible key usage.
privacy_boundary: No token values, credentials, private claims, health material, or personal source content are retained.
